meta.reasoning: As Good leader on Quest 1 (size 2), I propose s1 (myself) and s3 — both confirmed Good players. Avoiding s2 and s4 who are Evil.
Team [s1, s3] contains no Evil players — rejecting would look suspicious without cause. Approving builds credibility for future quests where I can position myself on the team to sabotage.

I am s3 (Good). The proposed team is [s1, s3] — I am on it. I approve because: (1) I can guarantee my own success vote, (2) Quest 1 is early game where Evil rarely sabotages to avoid suspicion, (3) rejecting without evidence would waste a proposal and risk the 5-rejection loss condition.

Team is [s1, s3] — both Good. Three approvals already in (s1, s2, s3), so team passes regardless. Voting reject is futile and suspicious. Voting approve maintains cover for future rounds where Evil needs influence over team selection.

result.summary: Evil wins by 5 consecutive proposal rejections! Score: Good 2 - Evil 1.
